description:
Letter from Lāmiʿ b. Ḥasan al-Kanzī to the Nubian eparch Uruwī ibn Khiyākh in Ibrīm, the Ikshīl and Master of the Horses. The letter shows an Egyptian official facilitating trade for Muslim merchants in Nubia and seeking the protection of local authorities for them. Lāmiʿ complains about the mistreatment of merchants traveling to Nubia. Every year, many boats travel to Nubia but are "badly mistreated" by the eparch's officials. This year, people from the port (of Aswan) faced two obstacles: injustice from locals and a shortage of goods. Lāmiʿ claims credit for alleviating these problems: "I took some haircutters (and opened sacks of produce) and gave relief to the people," with Nubians being the first beneficiaries. He asserts that without his intervention, "they would not have found produce, nor did anybody dare send (produce)." He mentions that he has personally sent two boats with his own goods, and requests protection for them: "I would like the Ikshīl to ensure that nobody obstructs them and that nobody diminishes what is rightly theirs." Lāmiʿ asks that if the boat crews need "pieces of karūk (calico cloth)," the eparch should provide them. Karūk was used as a form of currency. MR

Alternate readings (ASE):
- in r8–9, instead of مجزز شعر, it is possible to read مخزن شعير ("a storehouse of barley"), as in doc. 36 (PGPID 40708) where the same sender uses the same phrase (fataḥtu makhzan) in the same context. 

Khan, Qaṣr Ibrīm doc. 5.
description_he: מכתב מלאמע בן חסן אל-כנזי לאפרכוס הנובי אורווי בן ח'יאח' באיברים, האיכשיל ואדון הסוסים. לאמע מתלונן על התנהגות הרעה כלפי סוחרים הנוסעים לנוביה. הוא טוען שהתערב כדי להקל את הבעיות ושלח שתי סירות עם סחורותיו, ומבקש הגנה עבורן: 'אני רוצה שהאיכשיל יוודא שאיש לא יעכבן ואיש לא יפחית ממה שמגיע להן בצדק'. מבקש גם שאם צוותי הסירות צריכים 'חתיכות קארוכ (בד קאליקו)', האפרכוס יספק אותם.
